title: aquilegia jonesii var. elatior
intro: Aquilegia jonesii var. elatior, also known as the taller Elwes' monk's hood or taller Elwes' columbine, is a variety of the Aquilegia jonesii plant, which is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Aquilegia jonesii var. elatior include its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its possible use in treating respiratory issues and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
